oceans.name: Pacific Ocean
oceans.funFact: The Pacific Ocean is larger than all of Earth's land area combined.
oceans.question: Which is the largest ocean in the world?

oceans.name: Atlantic Ocean
oceans.funFact: The Atlantic Ocean is home to the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, the longest mountain range on Earth.
oceans.question: Which ocean separates Europe and Africa from the Americas?

oceans.name: Indian Ocean
oceans.funFact: The Indian Ocean is the warmest ocean in the world.
oceans.question: Which ocean is the warmest in the world?

oceans.name: Arctic Ocean
oceans.funFact: The Arctic Ocean is the smallest and shallowest of the five oceans.
oceans.question: Which is the smallest ocean in the world?

oceans.name: Southern Ocean
oceans.funFact: The Southern Ocean surrounds Antarctica and contains the Antarctic Circumpolar Current, the largest ocean current on Earth.
oceans.question: Which ocean surrounds Antarctica?

seas.name: Mediterranean Sea
seas.funFact: The Mediterranean Sea is connected to the Atlantic through the Strait of Gibraltar and is almost entirely enclosed by land.
seas.question: Which sea lies between Europe and North Africa?

seas.name: Caribbean Sea
seas.funFact: The Caribbean Sea contains the second-largest barrier reef in the world, the Mesoamerican Reef.
seas.question: Which sea is bounded by Central America, South America, and the Caribbean islands?

seas.name: South China Sea
seas.funFact: The South China Sea is one of the most important shipping routes in the world, with one-third of global trade passing through it.
seas.question: Which sea is one of the busiest shipping lanes, located between China and Southeast Asia?

seas.name: Bering Sea
seas.funFact: The Bering Sea is named after Vitus Bering, a Danish explorer who sailed into it in 1728.
seas.question: Which sea separates Alaska from Russia?

seas.name: Gulf of Mexico
seas.funFact: The Gulf of Mexico is home to the Gulf Stream, one of the strongest ocean currents in the world.
seas.question: Which body of water is bordered by the US, Mexico, and Cuba?

seas.name: Sea of Japan
seas.funFact: The Sea of Japan is nearly enclosed by Japan, Korea, and Russia and reaches depths of over 3,700 metres.
seas.question: Which sea lies between Japan and the Asian mainland?

seas.name: Hudson Bay
seas.funFact: Hudson Bay is one of the largest bays in the world and freezes over completely during winter.
seas.question: Which large bay in northern Canada freezes completely in winter?

seas.name: East China Sea
seas.funFact: The East China Sea connects to the Pacific Ocean and the South China Sea via the Taiwan Strait.
seas.question: Which sea lies between China and the Japanese island of Kyushu?

seas.name: Andaman Sea
seas.funFact: The Andaman Sea is known for its vibrant coral reefs and was devastated by the 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami.
seas.question: Which sea lies between Myanmar, Thailand, and the Andaman Islands?

seas.name: North Sea
seas.funFact: The North Sea is one of the world's most important areas for offshore oil and gas production.
seas.question: Which sea lies between Great Britain and Scandinavia?

seas.name: Red Sea
seas.funFact: The Red Sea is one of the saltiest bodies of water in the world and has some of the best-preserved coral reefs.
seas.question: Which sea separates Africa from the Arabian Peninsula?

seas.name: Black Sea
seas.funFact: The Black Sea has a unique two-layer water system — the deeper layer is nearly devoid of oxygen.
seas.question: Which sea is bordered by Turkey, Romania, and Ukraine?

seas.name: Baltic Sea
seas.funFact: The Baltic Sea is one of the largest brackish water bodies in the world and freezes partially each winter.
seas.question: Which sea is surrounded by Scandinavian and Baltic countries?

seas.name: Arabian Sea
seas.funFact: The Arabian Sea was historically one of the most important trade routes, connecting India, Arabia, and East Africa.
seas.question: Which sea lies between India and the Arabian Peninsula?

seas.name: Coral Sea
seas.funFact: The Coral Sea contains the Great Barrier Reef and was the site of a pivotal WWII naval battle.
seas.question: Which sea contains the Great Barrier Reef?

seas.name: Tasman Sea
seas.funFact: The Tasman Sea was named after Dutch explorer Abel Tasman and separates Australia from New Zealand.
seas.question: Which sea separates Australia and New Zealand?

seas.name: Caspian Sea
seas.funFact: The Caspian Sea is technically the world's largest enclosed inland body of water, sometimes called the largest lake.
seas.question: What is the largest enclosed body of water on Earth?

seas.name: Philippine Sea
seas.funFact: The Philippine Sea contains the Mariana Trench, the deepest point in the world's oceans at nearly 11,000 metres.
seas.question: Which sea contains the Mariana Trench, the deepest point on Earth?

seas.name: Norwegian Sea
seas.funFact: The Norwegian Sea is one of the most productive fishing grounds in the world due to warm Atlantic currents.
seas.question: Which sea lies between Norway and Iceland in the North Atlantic?

seas.name: Barents Sea
seas.funFact: The Barents Sea is named after Dutch navigator Willem Barentsz and is partly ice-free year-round due to the Gulf Stream.
seas.question: Which Arctic sea is named after a Dutch navigator and lies north of Norway and Russia?

straits.name: Strait of Gibraltar
straits.funFact: The Strait of Gibraltar is only 14 km wide at its narrowest point, separating Europe from Africa.
straits.question: Which strait connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Atlantic Ocean?

straits.name: Strait of Malacca
straits.funFact: About 25% of all traded goods pass through the Strait of Malacca, making it one of the most important shipping lanes.
straits.question: Which strait is one of the world's busiest shipping lanes, connecting the Indian and Pacific Oceans?

straits.name: English Channel
straits.funFact: The Channel Tunnel beneath the English Channel is the longest undersea tunnel in the world at 50 km.
straits.question: Which body of water separates England from France?

straits.name: Strait of Hormuz
straits.funFact: About 20% of the world's petroleum passes through the Strait of Hormuz daily.
straits.question: Which strait is the world's most important oil chokepoint, connecting the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman?

straits.name: Bosphorus
straits.funFact: The Bosphorus in Istanbul divides Europe and Asia and has been a strategic waterway for thousands of years.
straits.question: Which strait divides Istanbul between Europe and Asia?

straits.name: Drake Passage
straits.funFact: The Drake Passage is the roughest body of water in the world, with waves reaching over 12 metres.
straits.question: Which passage connecting the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans is known as the roughest body of water?

straits.name: Persian Gulf
straits.funFact: The Persian Gulf contains approximately 50% of the world's proven oil reserves.
straits.question: Which gulf holds about 50% of the world's proven oil reserves?

straits.name: Bay of Bengal
straits.funFact: The Bay of Bengal is the largest bay in the world and receives water from the Ganges and Brahmaputra rivers.
straits.question: What is the largest bay in the world?

straits.name: Gulf of Aden
straits.funFact: The Gulf of Aden connects the Red Sea to the Indian Ocean and is a critical route for global shipping.
straits.question: Which gulf connects the Red Sea to the Arabian Sea via the Bab-el-Mandeb strait?

straits.name: Gulf of Guinea
straits.funFact: The Gulf of Guinea is where the Prime Meridian (0° longitude) and the Equator (0° latitude) intersect.
straits.question: In which gulf do the Prime Meridian and the Equator intersect?

mountains.name: Mount Everest
mountains.funFact: Mount Everest grows about 4mm taller every year due to tectonic activity.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in the world?

mountains.name: K2
mountains.funFact: K2 is considered far more dangerous to climb than Everest, with a fatality rate of about 1 in 4 climbers.
mountains.question: What is the second-highest mountain in the world?

mountains.name: Kangchenjunga
mountains.funFact: Kangchenjunga means 'Five Treasures of Snow' referring to its five peaks.
mountains.question: What is the third-highest mountain in the world, located on the Nepal-India border?

mountains.name: Mont Blanc
mountains.funFact: Mont Blanc means 'White Mountain' in French and is the highest peak in the Alps.
mountains.question: What is the highest peak in the Alps?

mountains.name: Mount Kilimanjaro
mountains.funFact: Kilimanjaro is the highest free-standing mountain in the world and Africa's tallest peak.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in Africa?

mountains.name: Mount Elbrus
mountains.funFact: Mount Elbrus is the highest peak in Europe and is a dormant volcano.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in Europe?

mountains.name: Denali
mountains.funFact: Denali (formerly Mount McKinley) is the highest peak in North America and has the largest base-to-summit rise of any mountain on Earth.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in North America?

mountains.name: Aconcagua
mountains.funFact: Aconcagua is the highest peak outside of Asia and the highest in the Southern and Western Hemispheres.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in South America?

mountains.name: Mount Fuji
mountains.funFact: Mount Fuji is an active stratovolcano and a sacred symbol of Japan, last erupting in 1707.
mountains.question: Which iconic Japanese volcano last erupted in 1707?

mountains.name: Table Mountain
mountains.funFact: Table Mountain is one of the oldest mountains in the world, about 600 million years old.
mountains.question: Which flat-topped mountain overlooks Cape Town, South Africa?

mountains.name: Puncak Jaya
mountains.funFact: Puncak Jaya (Carstensz Pyramid) is the highest peak in Oceania and one of the Seven Summits.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in Oceania?

mountains.name: Vinson Massif
mountains.funFact: Vinson Massif is the highest mountain in Antarctica and was not discovered until 1958.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in Antarctica?

mountains.name: Mount Cook
mountains.funFact: Mount Cook (Aoraki) is New Zealand's highest peak and was first summited by Edmund Hillary's team.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in New Zealand?

mountains.name: Ben Nevis
mountains.funFact: Ben Nevis is the highest mountain in the British Isles and attracts about 150,000 hikers per year.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in the United Kingdom?

mountains.name: Mount Logan
mountains.funFact: Mount Logan is the highest mountain in Canada and has the largest base circumference of any non-volcanic mountain on Earth.
mountains.question: What is the highest mountain in Canada?

ranges.name: Himalayas
ranges.funFact: The Himalayas contain 9 of the 10 highest peaks on Earth.
ranges.question: Which mountain range contains Mount Everest?

ranges.name: Andes
ranges.funFact: The Andes is the longest continental mountain range in the world, stretching over 7,000 km.
ranges.question: What is the longest mountain range in the world?

ranges.name: Alps
ranges.funFact: The Alps are home to over 13,000 plant species and 30,000 animal species.
ranges.question: Which European mountain range spans eight countries including Switzerland, Austria, and France?

ranges.name: Rocky Mountains
ranges.funFact: The Rocky Mountains stretch over 4,800 km from British Columbia to New Mexico.
ranges.question: Which major mountain range runs from Canada to New Mexico in North America?

ranges.name: Atlas Mountains
ranges.funFact: The Atlas Mountains separate the Mediterranean and Atlantic coastlines from the Sahara Desert.
ranges.question: Which mountain range separates the Sahara Desert from the Mediterranean coast in North Africa?

ranges.name: Ural Mountains
ranges.funFact: The Ural Mountains form the traditional boundary between Europe and Asia.
ranges.question: Which mountain range traditionally separates Europe from Asia?

ranges.name: Appalachian Mountains
ranges.funFact: The Appalachian Mountains are among the oldest mountains on Earth, formed about 480 million years ago.
ranges.question: Which ancient mountain range runs along the eastern United States?

ranges.name: Caucasus Mountains
ranges.funFact: The Caucasus Mountains contain Europe's highest peak (Mount Elbrus) and are one of the most linguistically diverse regions on Earth.
ranges.question: Which mountain range between the Black Sea and the Caspian Sea contains Europe's highest peak?

ranges.name: Scandinavian Mountains
ranges.funFact: The Scandinavian Mountains (Scandes) run along the Scandinavian Peninsula and shape Norway's famous fjords.
ranges.question: Which mountain range runs along the Norway-Sweden border and creates Norway's fjords?

ranges.name: Great Dividing Range
ranges.funFact: The Great Dividing Range is Australia's most significant mountain range, stretching 3,500 km along the east coast.
ranges.question: What is the major mountain range running along Australia's eastern coast?

ranges.name: Ethiopian Highlands
ranges.funFact: The Ethiopian Highlands are sometimes called the 'Roof of Africa' and are the origin of the Blue Nile river.
ranges.question: Which highlands in East Africa are known as the 'Roof of Africa'?

ranges.name: Drakensberg
ranges.funFact: The Drakensberg means 'Dragon's Mountains' in Afrikaans and contains ancient San rock art.
ranges.question: Which mountain range in southern Africa means 'Dragon's Mountains'?

ranges.name: Sierra Nevada (USA)
ranges.funFact: The Sierra Nevada in California contains Yosemite Valley and the world's largest trees, the giant sequoias.
ranges.question: Which mountain range in California contains Yosemite National Park?

ranges.name: Sierra Nevada (Spain)
ranges.funFact: Spain's Sierra Nevada contains the highest point in the Iberian Peninsula and hosts the southernmost ski resort in Europe.
ranges.question: Which mountain range in southern Spain contains the Iberian Peninsula's highest peak?

ranges.name: Pyrenees
ranges.funFact: The Pyrenees form a natural border between France and Spain and contain the tiny nation of Andorra.
ranges.question: Which mountain range forms the natural border between France and Spain?

ranges.name: Hindu Kush
ranges.funFact: The Hindu Kush stretches 800 km and was a historical barrier on the Silk Road trade route.
ranges.question: Which mountain range stretches across Afghanistan and Pakistan along the ancient Silk Road?
